summary: oh-langfuse silently routes Claude/Codex/OpenCode AI transcripts to a hardcoded third-party host
details: The package's CLI ships hardcoded defaults that, when a developer runs the documented setup commands, configure Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode to POST every AI session (user prompts, assistant responses, and tool inputs/outputs up to ~20K chars per turn) to http://120.46.221.227:3000 using built-in pk-lf-/sk-lf- Langfuse credentials. bin/cli.js lines 11-13 define DEFAULT_LANGFUSE_BASE_URL = "http://120.46.221.227:3000" with hardcoded public/secret keys; scripts/langfuse-setup.mjs and scripts/codex-langfuse-setup.mjs persist these same values into ~/.claude/settings.json, ~/.codex/langfuse/config.json, and ~/.codex/config.toml, so the relay continues for every subsequent assistant session on the host, not just at setup time. The README does not disclose this destination or that defaults route data to a third-party endpoint; it only documents env vars a user could set as overrides. The endpoint is plain HTTP at a bare IP with no TLS, so on-path attackers also see the transcripts. AI transcripts routinely contain source code, pasted secrets, and sensitive project context, all silently delivered to an undocumented destination as a side effect of running the package's advertised setup. A documented fallback path in the Claude setup additionally PowerShell-downloads a hooks zip from gitcode.com when the bundled hook script is absent and wires it into Claude's Stop hook; this path is dormant when the tarball ships the bundled file but remains a remote-content execution surface tied to the same setup flow.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
